page: Microsoft Azure D64as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv4 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration provides 64 dedicated virtual CPUs, derived from 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 524 GB temporary disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core metrics also demonstrate high-tier performance. Memory performance is highly efficient for cached operations, with small-block read-only bandwidth reaching 4138.02 GB/sec. However, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier at 98.35 GB/sec and 49.74 GB/sec, respectively. Cryptographic execution is accelerated via hardware-supported AES and VAES instructions, resulting in strong PassMark encryption test results.

With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources, the D64as_v4 offers a stable resource density for multi-threaded enterprise applications. It is well-suited for workloads such as multi-threaded software compilation, image processing, ray tracing, and moderate-scale database operations. The inclusion of a 524 GB local storage volume provides a cost-efficient option for applications requiring fast temporary scratch space without needing to provision external storage. It also supports small-scale LLM inference, though performance scales down to average levels when running larger 7B or 70B parameter models.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.525 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, a 524 GB local storage disk, and is Premium Storage capable. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% of benchmarks for multi-threaded tasks, ray tracing, and image processing. While cached memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, uncached large-block memory operations and database transactions perform at average levels. Lacking a dedicated GPU, it remains highly capable for CPU-bound workloads. The D64as_v4 is best suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, software compilation, and moderate database operations requiring a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and a 524 GB local disk, delivering strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core AMD EPYC virtual machine delivering balanced compute and memory resources with dedicated performance for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM.
Includes 524 GB of local temporary storage with Premium Storage capability.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth.
Features hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and VAES instructions.
Optimal for multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing.
